thread.title: Anarchists tried to recapture Nikos Romanos at the Evelipidon courthouse
thread.title_full: Anarchists tried to recapture Nikos Romanos at the Evelipidon courthouse

author: admin
title: Anarchists tried to recapture Nikos Romanos at the Evelipidon courthouse
text:
The anarchists tried to recapture N. Romanos at the Evelpidon courthouse when the famous terrorist was brought to select a preventive measure for him in the case of the explosion in Ambelokipi.
[Nikos Romanos: what does his arrest warrant say?](https://rua.gr/news/sobmn/67557-nikos-romanos-chto-govoritsya-v-ego-ordere-na-arest.html)
On Friday, a well-known Greek terrorist was brought to the investigator's office to give evidence in the case of an explosion in an apartment on Arcadia Street, in Ambelokipi. Nikos Romanos faces the following charges:
As a result, clashes occurred between supporters of Nikos Romanos and the MAT police detachment. The police used tear gas grenades and stun grenades.
Στη φυλακή ο Νίκος
[#Ρωμανός].
Τα ρεμάλια της Αριστεράς βγήκαν να κάψουν την Αθήνα.Σας έχει σιχαθεί ο κόσμος…
[pic.twitter.com/hLvfrTt91i]— Νίκος??Σωτηρόπουλος (@NIKSOTIROPOYLOS)
[November 22, 2024]
Police attack on people gathered in Evelpidon prison for NIKO ROMANO. People who tried to leave the court building were pushed by the police and the police attacked them with chemical gas and sound bombs.
[#Antireport][#Ρωμανος][#Greece][#Athens][pic.twitter.com/dwy85IDCwl]— Partizan Yunanistan (@partizanGreece1)
[November 22, 2024]
[Nikos Romanos: what does his arrest warrant say?](https://rua.gr/news/sobmn/67557-nikos-romanos-chto-govoritsya-v-ego-ordere-na-arest.html)
On Friday, a well-known Greek terrorist was brought to the investigator's office to give evidence in the case of an explosion in an apartment on Arcadia Street, in Ambelokipi. Nikos Romanos faces the following charges:
- creation and participation in a terrorist organization with aggravating circumstances, including joint production, supply and storage of weapons and explosives for the purposes of a terrorist organization;
- terrorist attack using explosive devices;
- supply and storage of explosives;
- explosion with possible intent.
“Athens News” reveals [details of his arrest](https://rua.gr/news/sobmn/67502-nikosa-romanosa-arestovali-za-vzryv-v-ambelokimi.html)which happened on Monday evening. “He is accused of having, within Attica, from an unknown time in 2024 until 11/1/2024, created and become a member of a structured and active group consisting of three or more persons who act together and seek to commit the crime of a terrorist organization.”the indictment says, after which the charges in connection with the explosion in the apartment are laid out sequentially.
According to authorities, Nikos Romanos, along with three other defendants (including a man who died in an apartment explosion in Ambelokipi), manufactured and stored an improvised explosive device that exploded at 14:39 on October 31, 2024.
“You jointly stored an improvised explosive device in an apartment on the third floor of a residential building on Arcadia Street, and it exploded due to improper handling,” – stated in the indictment.
Apartment in Ambelokipi where the explosion occurred
The indictment states that they possessed large quantities of explosives and flammable substances, and also illegally possessed weapons. Regarding his fingerprint found on a plastic bag, the indictment states:
“There is strong evidence that the accused has committed criminal acts as part of the fingerprint found on the outer surface of a black and blue plastic bag with white laces was found to match the fingerprint of the accused's ring finger. The following were found wrapped and seized inside a plastic bag: 1 black CZ pistol and one metal magazine without cartridges.”
The indictment also mentions his criminal history, namely that he was sentenced to 18 years and 4 months for similar crimes, and ends by stating that ““If he remains at large, there is a high likelihood that he will commit other serious crimes.”
Nikos Romanos, for his part, prepares his testimony and insists that that he had nothing to do with the case and does not know any of the accused.
